LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE CONVERSION CLAIM SUPPORTS THE PRAYER FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES AND IT CANNOT BE DETERMINED, AS A PLEADING MATTER, WHETHER PARAGRAPH 18 REFERS TO MATTERS THAT ARE INADMISSIBLE UNDER EVIDENCE CODE 1152. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint; Memo Of P&A
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Diane M. Coe, Esq., and Constance Morrison, Esq., from Jackson, Jenkins Renstrom LLP, 818-379-4700, for defendant and moving party
Mark E. McKeen, Esq., from Paul Hastings LLP, 415-856-7075, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiff is given leave to delete the accounting cause of action and plead facts to support a constructive trust. Plaintiff's request is supported by the strong policy favoring amendments to pleadings and the lack of any prejudice to defendant.
The court was informed that the motion to continue trial date was withdrawn.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Diane M. Coe, Esq., and Constance Morrison, Esq., from Jackson, Jenkins Renstrom LLP, 818-379-4700, for defendant and moving party
Mark E. McKeen, Esq., from Paul Hastings LLP, 415-856-7075, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the Side Agreement is void for a failure of consideration. Even if J&W never obtained the tail insurance, it provided a benefit to JJR by assigning the Fireman's Fund account to JJR. JJR also fails to demonstrate that the Side Agreement was ever rescinded. Plaintiff has not split its causes of actions. Plaintiff's claims against J&W are based on primary rights that are totally distinct from its claims against JJR. As to the third cause of action for breach of contract, while it is arguable whether plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary of the Side Agreement, summary adjudication is denied per Code of Civil Procedure 437c(h). Deponents Jackson and Renstrom have information regarding the parties' intent when they executed the Side Agreement yet they failed to appear for their depositions without substantial justification. Defendant fails to satisfy its burden of production on the conversion claim that it is not retaining funds owed to plaintiff. Defendant also fails to satisfy its burden on the request for punitive damages because the request is supported by the conversion claim. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. BELLA S. SATRA OF PAUL HASTINGS, TEL. NO. 856-7043 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Denied. The conversion claim supports the prayer for exemplary damages and it cannot be determined, as a pleading matter, whether paragraph 18 refers to matters that are inadmissible under Evidence Code 1152. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
